+title: 'SchemaConverter: JSON Schema structure ŌåÆ DynamicGenerationSchema (+ corpus)'
+---
+## What
+Create `Sources/FoundationModelsMCP/SchemaConverter.swift`: pure conversion from an MCP `inputSchema` (`MCP.Value` carrying JSON Schema 2020-12) to `DynamicGenerationSchema` ŌåÆ `GenerationSchema(root:dependencies:)`. Cover the structure table in plan.md: `type: object`+`properties`, `required`ŌåÆnon-optional `Property`, string/integer/number/boolean, `array`+`items`, `enum`ŌåÆanyOf choices, nested objects, `$ref`/`$defs`ŌåÆnamed schema + `dependencies:`. Unknown/unsupported keywords fall to the permissive-type fallback (a later task adds guides + logging; here just don't throw). Also add a fixtures corpus `Tests/FoundationModelsMCPTests/Fixtures/*.json` of real MCP inputSchemas (filesystem-server, github-server style tools).
+
+- [ ] Object/required/primitive/array/enum/nested mapping
+- [ ] $ref/$defs ŌåÆ dependencies
+- [ ] Corpus fixture files (Ōēź8 real-world schemas)
+- [ ] Graceful handling of unknown keywords (no throw)
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] Every structure row of plan.md's schema-translation table is implemented
+- [ ] All corpus fixtures convert without throwing
+- [ ] Property names, optionality, and nesting verified against fixtures
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `Tests/FoundationModelsMCPTests/SchemaConverterStructureTests.swift`: table-driven over the corpus asserting names, required/optional, nesting, and $ref resolution
+- [ ] `swift test --filter SchemaConverterStructure` green
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` ŌĆö write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

## Review Findings (2026-07-03 16:33)

- [ ] `Sources/FoundationModelsMCP/SchemaConverter.swift:186` ŌĆö Four primitive-type cases in the parseNode switch statement ("string", "integer", "number", "boolean") are verbatim duplicates, each following the identical pattern `case "X": return .X`. Duplicates inflate maintenance surface and risk drift if logic changes. Extract type mapping into a helper function like `private static func primitiveSchemaIR(_ typeString: String) -> SchemaIR?` that centralizes the string-to-SchemaIR conversion, then eliminate the duplicate cases by calling it.
- [ ] `Sources/FoundationModelsMCP/SchemaConverter.swift:256` ŌĆö Three cases in the scalarString switch statement (case .int, .double, .bool) are near-verbatim duplicates, each following the identical pattern `case .X(let x): return String(x)`. Duplicates create maintenance burden and risk drift if the conversion logic needs to change. Extract the numeric/boolean-to-string conversion logic into a helper function or consolidate these cases, as they all perform the same String(...) conversion operation on different types.
- [ ] `Sources/FoundationModelsMCP/SchemaConverter.swift:282` ŌĆö Four primitive type cases (.string, .integer, .number, .boolean) are near-verbatim duplicates that differ only in the type parameter. Each follows the identical pattern `return DynamicGenerationSchema(type: X.self)`. Duplicates inflate the maintenance surface and risk drift if one case is modified but others aren't. Extract a helper function parameterized by type: `private static func primitiveSchema(_ type: Any.Type) -> DynamicGenerationSchema { DynamicGenerationSchema(type: type) }`, then invoke it from each case: `case .string: return primitiveSchema(String.self)`, etc.

-## What

Cover the structure table in plan.md: `type: object`+`properties`, `required`ŌåÆnon-optional, primitives, `array`+`items`, `enum`, nested objects, `$ref`/`$defs`ŌåÆnamed schema + `dependencies:`. Unknown keywords fall through gracefully (no throw; guides/logging arrive in the follow-on task). Add corpus fixtures `Tests/FoundationModelsMCPTests/Fixtures/*.json` (Ōēź8 real-world MCP schemas).

- [x] SchemaIR (inspectable, Sendable) + parser
- [x] IR ŌåÆ DynamicGenerationSchema emission (thin)
- [x] Structure table fully mapped; $ref/$defs resolved
- [x] Corpus fixtures; unknown keywords tolerated

- `dynamicSchema(for:)`'s five primitive-only arms (`.string`, `.integer`, `.number`, `.boolean`, `.unknown`) were replaced by a `private static let primitiveDynamicSchemas: [(SchemaIR, DynamicGenerationSchema)]` table of precomputed `(case, schema)` pairs, looked up by `Equatable` match in a single `default:` arm (with the `.object`/`.array`/`.enumeration`/`.reference` cases ŌĆö which have real per-case logic, not constant-only differences ŌĆö left as explicit switch arms). Replace the switch with a static dictionary that maps type strings to SchemaIR cases: `let primitiveTypeMap: [String: SchemaIR] = ["string": .string, "integer": .integer, "number": .number, "boolean": .boolean]`, then return `primitiveTypeMap[typeString]` in the function body.
- [x] `Sources/FoundationModelsMCP/SchemaConverter.swift:286` ŌĆö Five consecutive primitive type cases in dynamicSchema() (.string, .integer, .number, .boolean, .unknown) form a switch over a known set whose arms differ only in the type argument to primitiveSchema(). This mapping should be expressed as lookup data, not as parallel code paths. Create a static dictionary or mapping that associates each primitive SchemaIR case with its corresponding Generable type, then use it to determine the type once per call instead of switching on each primitive case separately.

### Fix applied (2026-07-03 21:55)

- `primitiveSchemaIR(_:)` now looks up a `private static let primitiveTypeMap: [String: SchemaIR]` dictionary instead of switching on the four primitive type strings.
- `dynamicSchema(for:)`'s five primitive-only arms (`.string`, `.integer`, `.number`, `.boolean`, `.unknown`) were replaced by a `private static let primitiveDynamicSchemas: [(SchemaIR, DynamicGenerationSchema)]` table of precomputed `(case, schema)` pairs, looked up by `Equatable` match in a single `default:` arm (with the `.object`/`.array`/`.enumeration`/`.reference` cases ŌĆö which have real per-case logic, not constant-only differences ŌĆö left as explicit switch arms). Chose a precomputed-value array over a dictionary keyed by the whole `SchemaIR` enum (not proposed since `SchemaIR` isn't `Hashable`) or a closure table (would need `@Sendable` closures under Swift 6 strict concurrency for the static `let`); the array-of-pairs form sidesteps both concerns since `SchemaIR` is already `Equatable` and `DynamicGenerationSchema` values are computed once at static-init time.

Verification: `swift build` ŌĆö exit 0, zero warnings. `swift test` ŌĆö 11/11 pass (Smoke + SchemaConverterStructure suites unchanged; pure internal refactor, no behavior change).

## Review Findings (2026-07-03 16:49)

- [x] `Sources/FoundationModelsMCP/SchemaConverter.swift:268` ŌĆö The switch statement in primitiveSchemaIR() maps four known JSON Schema primitive type strings to SchemaIR cases with only constant differences between arms. This should be expressed as a lookup table (dictionary) interpreted by one code path, not as parallel switch case branches that a human must keep in lockstep. Replace the switch with a static dictionary that maps type strings to SchemaIR cases: `let primitiveTypeMap: [String: SchemaIR] = [\"string\": .string, \"integer\": .integer, \"number\": .number, \"boolean\": .boolean]`, then return `primitiveTypeMap[typeString]` in the function body.
- [x] `Sources/FoundationModelsMCP/SchemaConverter.swift:286` ŌĆö Five consecutive primitive type cases in dynamicSchema() (.string, .integer, .number, .boolean, .unknown) form a switch over a known set whose arms differ only in the type argument to primitiveSchema(). This mapping should be expressed as lookup data, not as parallel code paths. Create a static dictionary or mapping that associates each primitive SchemaIR case with its corresponding Generable type, then use it to determine the type once per call instead of switching on each primitive case separately.

### Fix applied (2026-07-03 21:55)

- `primitiveSchemaIR(_:)` now looks up a `private static let primitiveTypeMap: [String: SchemaIR]` dictionary instead of switching on the four primitive type strings.
- `dynamicSchema(for:)`'s five primitive-only arms (`.string`, `.integer`, `.number`, `.boolean`, `.unknown`) were replaced by a `private static let primitiveDynamicSchemas: [(SchemaIR, DynamicGenerationSchema)]` table of precomputed `(case, schema)` pairs, looked up by `Equatable` match in a single `default:` arm (with the `.object`/`.array`/`.enumeration`/`.reference` cases ŌĆö which have real per-case logic, not constant-only differences ŌĆö left as explicit switch arms). Chose a precomputed-value array over a dictionary keyed by the whole `SchemaIR` enum (not proposed since `SchemaIR` isn't `Hashable`) or a closure table (would need `@Sendable` closures under Swift 6 strict concurrency for the static `let`); the array-of-pairs form sidesteps both concerns since `SchemaIR` is already `Equatable` and `DynamicGenerationSchema` values are computed once at static-init time.

Verification: `swift build` ŌĆö exit 0, zero warnings. `swift test` ŌĆö 11/11 pass (Smoke + SchemaConverterStructure suites unchanged; pure internal refactor, no behavior change).

## Review Findings (2026-07-03 16:49)

- [x] `Sources/FoundationModelsMCP/SchemaConverter.swift:268` ŌĆö The switch statement in primitiveSchemaIR() maps four known JSON Schema primitive type strings to SchemaIR cases with only constant differences between arms. This should be expressed as a lookup table (dictionary) interpreted by one code path, not as parallel switch case branches that a human must keep in lockstep. Replace the switch with a static dictionary that maps type strings to SchemaIR cases: `let primitiveTypeMap: [String: SchemaIR] = [\"string\": .string, \"integer\": .integer, \"number\": .number, \"boolean\": .boolean]`, then return `primitiveTypeMap[typeString]` in the function body.
- [x] `Sources/FoundationModelsMCP/SchemaConverter.swift:286` ŌĆö Five consecutive primitive type cases in dynamicSchema() (.string, .integer, .number, .boolean, .unknown) form a switch over a known set whose arms differ only in the type argument to primitiveSchema(). This mapping should be expressed as lookup data, not as parallel code paths. Create a static dictionary or mapping that associates each primitive SchemaIR case with its corresponding Generable type, then use it to determine the type once per call instead of switching on each primitive case separately.

### Fix applied (2026-07-03 21:55)

- `primitiveSchemaIR(_:)` now looks up a `private static let primitiveTypeMap: [String: SchemaIR]` dictionary instead of switching on the four primitive type strings.
- `dynamicSchema(for:)`'s five primitive-only arms (`.string`, `.integer`, `.number`, `.boolean`, `.unknown`) were replaced by a `private static let primitiveDynamicSchemas: [(SchemaIR, DynamicGenerationSchema)]` table of precomputed `(case, schema)` pairs, looked up by `Equatable` match in a single `default:` arm (with the `.object`/`.array`/`.enumeration`/`.reference` cases ŌĆö which have real per-case logic, not constant-only differences ŌĆö left as explicit switch arms). Chose a precomputed-value array over a dictionary keyed by the whole `SchemaIR` enum (not proposed since `SchemaIR` isn't `Hashable`) or a closure table (would need `@Sendable` closures under Swift 6 strict concurrency for the static `let`); the array-of-pairs form sidesteps both concerns since `SchemaIR` is already `Equatable` and `DynamicGenerationSchema` values are computed once at static-init time.
